title: What’s the On-Time Delivery Rate?
bodyHtml:
Jump to
What’s the On-Time Shipping / Delivery Rate?
How do we calculate this rate?
Where can I find my rate?
How can I improve my On-Time Shipping / Delivery Rate?  


 

What’s the On-Time Shipping Rate / Delivery Rate?

The On-Time Shipping (OTS) & On-Time Delivery (OTD) KPIs have been bundled together in a Shipping & Delivery rates block.

On-Time Shipping (OTS) reflects your ability to prepare an order within the required timeframe.

On-Time Delivery Rate (OTD)  reflects your ability to deliver an order to your customer within the required timeframe. 

These rates track the number of orders tracked by Back Market tools within the last 30 days.

If your rate within a particular market is among the lowest-performing sellers on the platform, Back Market will suspend your listings in this market for 1 week.
 

How do we calculate this rate?

OTS : We divide the number of orders supposed to be shipped in the past 30 days by the total number of orders supposed to be shipped in the last 30 days. 


OTD : We divide the number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the last 30 days. 
 

Where can I find my rate?

To see your rate, log into your Back Office > “Insights” > “Quality.”

Here you’ll see your results and how you compare to the Back Market seller average.


 

To better understand your performance, select the “See details” button. 

Your BackBox performance is currently only based on On Time Delivery. Keep in mind that the better your On-Time Delivery Rate is, the higher the prices you’ll be able to set to win BackBoxes, thus increasing your margins.
Please also note that On Time Shipping has an important impact on Customer Delivery Contacts and therefore on CCBM fees. 

 

How can I improve my On-Time Delivery Rate? 

Please refer to the Quality Charter to have visibility on Shipping / Delivery deadlines that are required per product categories. 

 

Here are some tips :

  • You can change your delivery speeds directly in your Back Office.
  • When you ship an order, you must add the tracking number and shipping provider in your Back Office. Make sure you add the correct shipping provider (the one also registered in your options).
  • If you add a shipping provider that doesn’t comply with your delivery options or the tracking number isn’t compatible with the shipping provider indicated, then our system will not be able to track your order. This will increase your Untracked Rate within the On-Time Delivery Rate and reduce your ability to win BackBoxes.
  • Remember that Back Market is here to support you and help your business perform as best as possible. We offer BackShip shipping services that can improve your delivery performance.
